items.name: 10-year Treasury yield
items.note: yield ticked back up slightly even after a cooler PPI print, as oil-driven inflation risk from the Iran conflict keeps some upward pressure

items.name: Crude oil (WTI)
items.note: holding near one-month highs as Middle East tensions keep a floor under prices

items.name: Gold (per oz)
items.note: slipping toward its lowest level since November as rate-cut hopes cool and oil-driven inflation risk cuts both ways

items.name: Nvidia
items.note: fell with the broader chip complex even as it announced new AI partnerships in Japan

items.name: Microsoft
items.note: climbing as analysts reaffirm its AI lead ahead of July 29 earnings

items.name: Alphabet
items.note: fell over 4% on reports its flagship Gemini 3.5 Pro model is delayed and falling short of internal coding-capability goals, plus new EU rules forcing it to open Android/search data to AI rivals

items.name: Oracle
items.note: extended its slide after S&P cut its credit rating to just one step above junk, citing its AI data-center spending spree; now down over 60% from its all-time high

items.name: Taiwan Semiconductor
items.note: fell alongside the broader chip complex despite reporting a record 77% jump in Q2 profit and raising its full-year outlook, plus a fresh $100B Arizona investment pledge

items.name: Micron Technology
items.note: extending a multi-day slide as investors rotate out of high-flying memory names; still up sharply YTD

items.name: ASML Holding
items.note: pulled back with equipment names a day after its second sales-guidance raise this year on AI chip demand

items.name: Tesla (Optimus)
items.note: steady ahead of July 22 Q2 earnings; Wall Street continues raising price targets after last week's delivery beat

items.name: Intuitive Surgical
items.note: jumped after beating Q2 estimates on strong da Vinci procedure growth (~16% YoY) and maintaining its full-year outlook

items.name: Teradyne
items.note: fell with the broader chip-equipment selloff ahead of its own July 28 earnings

items.name: Salesforce
items.note: bounced back as investors rotate into beaten-down enterprise software names after last week's Agentforce-driven downgrades

items.name: ServiceNow
items.note: still digesting the IBM-driven software pullback ahead of its own July 22 earnings

items.name: Adobe
items.note: jumped nearly 5% as bulls push back on AI-disruption fears citing its own growing AI revenue

items.name: Ouster (lidar)
items.note: pulling back after a recent public share offering and a strong multi-week run

items.name: Vishay Precision Group
items.note: reliable same-day quote unavailable; last confirmed close was $107.56 (Jul 15)

items.name: Cadence Design Systems
items.note: eased despite launching a new agentic AI design platform this week; reports earnings July 27

items.name: Bitcoin
items.note: holding near recent highs as markets await a Clarity Act hearing in Congress on crypto market-structure rules

items.name: Ethereum
items.note: outperforming Bitcoin, with some analysts calling it 'increasingly compelling' amid the regulatory-clarity backdrop

items.name: CrowdStrike
items.note: picked up its fourth Buy rating this week (Citi raised its target to $250) even as shares eased slightly today; still well above its tracked stop

items.name: Zscaler
items.note: giving back some of its recent rally; now trading closer to its tracked entry after KeyBanc raised its target on strong survey checks

items.name: ServiceNow
items.note: still below its pre-selloff level

outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: CrowdStrike picked up its fourth analyst Buy rating in a week, with Citi raising its price target to $250, as the sector keeps benefiting from AI-driven threat concerns and enterprise budget reallocation echoing IBM's recent commentary on client spending shifts.

Intuitive Surgical beat Q2 estimates on strong da Vinci procedure growth and maintained its full-year outlook, while Nvidia expanded partnerships with Japanese robotics firms (Fanuc, Yaskawa) and unveiled a new Cosmos 3 Edge model for robot deployment — two separate signals pointing to durable physical-AI demand.

weakest.reason: A broad AI chip sell-off hit Nvidia, Broadcom, AMD, Arm, Teradyne and memory names even as TSMC reported a record 77% jump in Q2 profit and raised its outlook — a 'sell the news' reaction that suggests some investor fatigue with the AI capex trade rather than a company-specific problem.

Oracle extended its slide after S&P cut its credit rating to one step above junk over its AI data-center spending, now down more than 60% from its all-time high, while Alphabet fell over 4% on reports its next flagship AI model is delayed and behind on coding capability — two different but simultaneous cracks in the 'AI infrastructure is unstoppable' narrative.

outlook.nextStep: The cybersecurity sector's steady analyst upgrades and Intuitive Surgical's earnings beat look like the more durable signals worth a closer look right now; the semiconductor sell-off despite TSMC's blowout quarter is better watched than acted on, since it's unclear yet whether it's healthy profit-taking or an early sign of broader AI-capex fatigue.

signals.title: PPI cools further but oil and yields stay elevated on Middle East tensions
signals.body: A softer-than-expected PPI report gave stocks a mixed reaction Wednesday into Thursday, with the S&P 500 outperforming the tech-heavy Nasdaq as a semiconductor selloff weighed on growth names. The 10-year Treasury yield ticked back up slightly to around 4.62%, and WTI crude held near one-month highs around $80 as the US-Iran standoff continues to underpin energy prices.
signals.watch: Whether the Iran-related oil premium fades or escalates, and whether cooling PPI/CPI data keeps translating into lower near-term rate-hike odds.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — the inflation and geopolitical threads are pulling markets in opposite directions right now.

signals.title: AI chip stocks sell off despite TSMC's record quarter
signals.body: TSMC reported a 77% jump in Q2 profit, beat consensus on revenue, and raised its 2026 outlook alongside a fresh $100B Arizona investment pledge — yet its stock and the broader chip complex (Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, Arm, Teradyne, Micron) all fell sharply the same day. Reporting attributes it to profit-taking and renewed questions about how much longer AI capex spending can accelerate.
signals.watch: Whether the pullback extends into next week's earnings from AMD, Teradyne and others, or proves to be a one- or two-day rotation.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — a 'sell the news' reaction on genuinely strong fundamentals is worth watching closely before drawing conclusions either way.

signals.title: Oracle's credit rating cut near junk as its AI spending outpaces its balance sheet
signals.body: S&P Global cut Oracle's credit rating to BBB-, just one notch above junk, citing the scale of its AI data-center capital spending relative to its balance sheet. The stock fell another 6% today, extending a slide that has now erased more than 60% of its value from its all-time high in September.
signals.watch: Any updated guidance from Oracle on data-center capex pacing, debt issuance, or customer concentration in upcoming disclosures.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — the debt and execution risk here looks like a company-specific overhang distinct from the broader AI trade.

signals.title: Alphabet falls on reports its next flagship AI model is delayed
signals.body: Alphabet shares dropped more than 4% after Bloomberg reported Gemini 3.5 Pro is running months behind schedule and falling short of internal goals, particularly on coding capability. The same day, EU regulators issued binding rules forcing Google to open up Android and search data to AI rivals like OpenAI.
signals.watch: Whether Alphabet addresses the Gemini timeline directly at its July 22 earnings call.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a specific, sourced execution concern is a different kind of signal than general AI-trade jitters.

signals.title: Beaten-down software names rotate higher: Salesforce, Adobe bounce
signals.body: Salesforce and Adobe both jumped roughly 3-5% today as investors rotated back into enterprise software names that had been hit hardest by AI-disruption fears and recent analyst downgrades. Adobe bulls are pushing back on the bear case by pointing to the company's own growing AI-linked revenue.
signals.watch: Whether this holds as a genuine valuation-reset bounce or fades once chip-sector sentiment stabilizes and capital rotates back.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look, but be mindful this follows a string of downgrades — the move could still be volatile in either direction.

signals.title: Nvidia deepens physical-AI push in Japan as Intuitive Surgical beats on procedure growth
signals.body: Nvidia announced new partnerships with Japanese robotics firms Fanuc and Yaskawa alongside a new Cosmos 3 Edge AI model for robot deployment, while separately Intuitive Surgical beat Q2 estimates with da Vinci procedure growth near 16% YoY and maintained its full-year guidance.
signals.watch: Whether other robotics and physical-AI names confirm similar demand strength in upcoming earnings.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — two independent data points (a hyperscaler partnership and a medtech earnings beat) both point toward durable physical-AI demand.

signals.title: CrowdStrike picks up its fourth Buy rating this week
signals.body: CrowdStrike shares eased slightly today but the stock has now received four separate analyst Buy ratings and price-target raises this week, including Citi lifting its target to $250, as the sector continues to benefit from AI-driven threat concerns and enterprise budget reallocation.
signals.watch: Whether the string of upgrades reflects genuine fundamental momentum or is largely priced in already given the stock's proximity to its all-time high.
signals.focus: Worth checking in on, but keep in mind much of the initial catalyst-driven move may already be reflected in the price.
